rationale: Title X is concise, clinically framed, and well aligned with JICE. It communicates the procedure, complication, incidence, effects, and prognosis without overloading the reader. Title Y includes more study design detail and the comparator, but it is too long and syntactically crowded for a manuscript title.
loser_weakness: Title Y tries to include too many specifics, which reduces readability and editorial appeal despite its high informativeness.
